enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each record's CHROM, POS, ID, REF, ALT, QUAL, FILTER, INFO, FORMAT, and sample GT values; header meta-information content (excluding order) must be identical

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line key-value pair order invariance
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for all parsed header entries; the order of key=value pairs inside ##INFO, ##FORMAT, ##FILTER structured lines must not affect the parsed representation

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: ALT permutation invariance of query methods on parsed records
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method — the per-record properties (is_snv, is_variant, is_het, is_phased, gt_type) must be invariant under ALT permutation because the same set of alleles and genotypes is present, just reordered.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines). R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.
A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).
R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.

enforced.mr_name: multi-allelic split with query method invariance
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each query method — splitting a multi-ALT record into individual records preserves per-sample variant classification (SNV status, variant/het status, filter status)

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).
R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.

enforced.mr_name: Reject file with ##fileformat not on first line
enforced.oracle: All SUTs must REJECT or CRASH when ##fileformat is not the first line; silent acceptance or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug. The error-consensus oracle tags each voter's response as ACCEPT/REJECT/CRASH/SILENT_SKIP.

evidence.quote: A single `fileformat' line is always required, must be the first line in the file, and details the VCF format version number.

enforced.mr_name: Reject record with GT allele index exceeding ALT allele count
enforced.oracle: All SUTs must REJECT or CRASH when a GT allele index references an ALT allele that does not exist (index > len(ALT)); silent acceptance or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug. The error-consensus oracle tags each voter's response as ACCEPT/REJECT/CRASH/SILENT_SKIP.

evidence.quote: The allele values are 0 for the reference allele (what is in the REF field), 1 for the first allele listed in ALT, 2 for the second allele list in ALT and so on.

enforced.mr_name: Reject INFO field with Number=A cardinality mismatch (extra value on biallelic record)
enforced.oracle: All SUTs must REJECT or CRASH when an INFO field declared Number=A has a value count that does not equal len(ALT); silent acceptance or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug. The error-consensus oracle tags each voter's response as ACCEPT/REJECT/CRASH/SILENT_SKIP.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column

enforced.oracle: After round-tripping through the SUT's writer (parse → write → parse), the POS field of every record must be identical to the original. The 1-based coordinate system must be preserved without off-by-one errors.

evidence.quote: * POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1.

enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where T is VCF→BCF→VCF round-trip; the canonical JSON must be identical since BCF is the binary equivalent of VCF with identical information content

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — BCF specification] Overall, the idea behind is BCF2 is simple. BCF2 is a binary, compressed equivalent of VCF that can be indexed with tabix and can be efficiently decoded from disk or streams.
[VCF v4.5 — BCF specification] In BCF2, the original VCF records are converted to binary and encoded as BGZF blocks.

enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where T shuffles ##meta lines (keeping ##fileformat first); the canonical JSON must be identical since meta lines other than ##fileformat may appear in any order

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — Meta-information lines] Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.
[VCF v4.5 — File format] A single `fileformat' line is always required, must be the first line in the file, and details the VCF format version number.

enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where T permutes key=value pairs inside structured ##INFO/##FORMAT/##FILTER meta-lines; implementations must not rely on field ordering within structured lines

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — Meta-information lines] Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.
[VCF v4.5 — The VCF specification] Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: Meta-line shuffle preserves variant query results
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method — shuffling meta-information lines (other than ##fileformat) is semantically a no-op per spec, so all per-record query methods must return identical values

evidence.quote: Meta-information lines are optional, but if they are present then they must be completely well-formed. Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.
Meta-information lines are optional, but if they are present then they must be completely well-formed. Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line key-value reorder preserves variant query results
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method — reordering key=value pairs inside structured ##INFO/##FORMAT/##FILTER meta-lines must not change parsed record semantics per spec

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.
Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for all parsed INFO field values; the semicolon-separated key=value pairs in the INFO column may appear in any order without changing semantics

evidence.quote: INFO keys must match the regular expression , please note that "1000G" is allowed as a special legacy value. Duplicate keys are not allowed. Arbitrary keys are permitted, although those listed in Table <ref> and described below are reserved (albeit optional).

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that INFO key=value pairs can be in any order, but since keys are identified by name and duplicates are forbidden, the order is semantically irrelevant.

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) after re-identifying samples by their column header IDs; sample values must be correctly reassociated with sample names regardless of column ordering

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: The VCF spec does not explicitly address sample column ordering; however, since samples are identified by the column header names in the #CHROM line, the column order is semantically irrelevant as long as sample-to-column mapping is preserved.

quarantine.mr_name: BCF header dictionary order invariance via round-trip
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) after BCF round-trip; the order of ##contig, ##INFO, ##FORMAT, ##FILTER dictionary entries must not affect the decoded VCF content

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: BCF dictionary ordering is implementation-defined per VCF v4.5 §6.2.1; parsers that rely on implicit index positions without consulting the IDX field may produce divergent output.

quarantine.mr_name: ALT allele permutation with consistent remapping of Number=A, Number=R, and GT fields
quarantine.oracle: After permuting ALT alleles and remapping GT indices and Number=A/Number=R INFO/FORMAT values accordingly, the parsed variant semantics must be identical: the same set of alternate alleles is present (just in different order), and all per-allele and per-genotype values are consistently remapped to match the new ordering.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines). R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.
A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).
R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.
ALT — alternate base(s): Comma-separated list of alternate non-reference alleles.

quarantine.mr_name: Split multi-allelic record into single-ALT records with consistent Number=A and GT remapping
quarantine.oracle: Splitting a multi-ALT record into one record per ALT allele (with Number=A values split per-record and GT indices remapped to 0/1) must produce the same set of variant alleles and genotype calls as the original multi-ALT record. The split form is semantically equivalent per VCF normalization conventions.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines).
A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).
ALT — alternate base(s): Comma-separated list of alternate non-reference alleles.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly define multi-allelic splitting as a canonical normalization; this MR relies on the convention that a multi-ALT record and its split form represent the same set of variant alleles.

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — the canonical normalized representation must be semantically identical; any parser that normalizes must recognize both forms as the same variant

evidence.quote: For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant; this padding base is not required (although it is permitted) e.g. for complex substitutions or other variants where all alleles have at least one base represented in their Strings.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says padding base is 'not required (although it is permitted)' for complex substitutions — this MAY/SHOULD ambiguity means some tools may pad while others do not, but both forms are valid

quarantine.mr_name: left_align_indel normalization invariance in homopolymer runs
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — a left-shifted indel in a homopolymer run represents the same biological variant; parsers compliant with normalization must recognize the equivalent form

evidence.quote: For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant; this padding base is not required (although it is permitted) e.g. for complex substitutions or other variants where all alleles have at least one base represented in their Strings.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly mandate left-alignment; this is a convention from Tan et al. 2015 that is widely adopted but not codified in the VCF v4.5 spec itself

quarantine.mr_name: Reject data line with missing mandatory fixed column (QUAL dropped)
quarantine.oracle: All SUTs must REJECT or CRASH on the malformed record; silent acceptance or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug. The error-consensus oracle tags each voter's response as ACCEPT/REJECT/CRASH/SILENT_SKIP.

evidence.quote: The mandatory header line names the 8 fixed, mandatory columns. These columns are as follows: #CHROM POS ID REF ALT QUAL FILTER INFO

quarantine.mr_name: Missing value round-trip invariance via equivalent FORMAT fields
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where T appends a FORMAT field filled with '.' to all samples; missing values are semantically equivalent to absent trailing fields per the spec

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — The VCF specification] If any of the fields is missing, it is replaced with the MISSING value. ... If a field contains a list of missing values, it can be represented either as a single MISSING value (`.') or as a list of missing values (e.g. `.,.,.' if the field was Number=3). Trailing fields can be dropped, with the exception of the GT field, which should always be present if specified in the FORMAT field.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says GT 'should' always be present if specified — this is a SHOULD, not a MUST, so some implementations may handle missing GT differently

quarantine.mr_name: INFO field key-value shuffle preserves variant query results
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method — shuffling semicolon-separated key=value entries in the INFO column is semantically a no-op; the INFO field is a set of key-value pairs with no defined ordering

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that INFO key=value pairs in data lines have no ordering requirement, but the structured meta-line ordering principle and common practice (bcftools, htslib) treat them as unordered.

quarantine.mr_name: Sample column permutation preserves variant query results
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method — sample column ordering is a presentation detail; per-record properties (is_snv, is_variant, etc.) are invariant under sample permutation

evidence.quote: It is recommended to respect the ordering as specified in the input VCF/BCF2 file, but parsers should not rely on a specific ordering.

ambiguity_flags: The BCF spec quote refers to dictionary ordering, not sample columns directly. However, sample columns are also not semantically ordered by the VCF spec — they are a set of individuals.
